name: HMS Beryl (T 34)
events: 9 Sep 1939 HMS Snapper (Lt. W.D.A. King, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These exercises included an A/S exercise with HMS Coral and HMS Moonstone (Lt. W.J.H. Moorman, RN) and later with HMS Jade and HMS Beryl. (1)
12 Sep 1939 HMS Snapper (Lt. W.D.A. King, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These exercises included A/S exercises with HMS Coral and HMS Beryl. (1)
14 Sep 1939 HMS Salmon (Lt.Cdr. E.O. Bickford, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Alexandria with HMS Coral and HMS Beryl. (2)
22 Sep 1939 HMS Snapper (Lt. W.D.A. King, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These exercises included an A/S exercise with HMS Jade and HMS Beryl. (1)
25 Sep 1939 HMS Snapper (Lt. W.D.A. King, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These exercises included an A/S exercise with HMS Jade and HMS Beryl. (1)

21 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. This included a practice attack on HMS Malaya (Capt. I.B.B. Tower, DSC, RN) and A/S exercises with HMS HMS Coral and HMS Beryl. (3)
25 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Scarborough (Cdr. J.H. Ruck-Keene, RN) and HMS Beryl. (3)
28 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Beryl and later with HMS Daring (Cdr. S.A. Cooper, RN). (3)
7 Jan 1941 HMS Upright (Lt. E.D. Norman, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Malta during which practice attacks were made on HMS Beryl (A/Cdr.(Retd.) J.C. Bird, DSC, RN) and HMS Jade (?). (4)
3 Apr 1941 Around 1845 B/3, HMS Upholder (Lt.Cdr. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) departed Malta for her 8th war patrol (6th in the Mediterranean). She was ordered to patrol off the east coast of Tunisia.
On departure two practice attacks were carried out on an A/S trawler. [This must have been either HMS Beryl, HMS Coral or HMS Jade.] (5)
26 Apr 1941 HMS Upright (Lt. R.S. Brookes, DSC, RN) conducted attack exercises off Malta during which HMS Beryl (A/Cdr.(Retd.) J.C. Bird, DSC, RN) acted as target.
Spare Commanding Officer Lt. Brookes had temporary taken command of HMS Upright as Lt. Norman was unfit and indeed for a rest. (6)
15 May 1941 Around 1935 B/15, HMS Upholder (Lt.Cdr. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) departed Malta for her 10th war patrol (8th in the Mediterranean). She was ordered to patrol south of the Straits of Messina.
On departure a practice attack was carried out on an A/S trawler. [This must have been either HMS Beryl, HMS Coral or HMS Jade.] (7)

15 Jun 1941 Around 1715 B/15, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) departed Malta for her 12th war patrol (10th in the Mediterranean). She was ordered to patrol between the Kerkennah shallows and Lampion Island in area 'W', between latitudes 34°58'N, and 35°45'W and longitudes 11°02'E and 12°08'E.
On leaving harbour an attack exercise was carried out during which HMS Beryl (?) served as target. Later another practice attack was made on HMS Abingdon (Lt. G.A. Simmers, RNR). (7)

17 Jul 1941 In the afternoon, HMS Upholder (Lt.Cdr. M.D. Wanklyn, RN), HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN), HMS Urge (Lt. E.P. Tomkinson, RN) and HMS Utmost (Lt.Cdr. R.D. Cayley, RN) conducted exercises off Malta with HMS Beryl (?). (8)
19 Jul 1941 HMS Unique (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Malta with HMS Encounter (Lt.Cdr. E.V.St.J. Morgan, RN) and and HMS Beryl (Mid. H.W. du Boisson, RNR).
Also two practice attacks were carried out on HMS Encounter. (9)

12 Sep 1941 Around 1700 B/12, HMS Utmost (Lt.Cdr. R.D. Cayley, DSO, RN) departed Malta for her 13th war patrol (10th in the Mediterranean). She had just returned to port after exercising in the morning when she was ordered to proceed to position 34°36'N, 12°12'E, near the Kerkennah shallows, to pick up the crew from a crashed Blenheim bomber.
Earlier in the afternoon exercises had been carried out of Malta. These included practice attacks on HMS Beryl (Mid. H.W. du Boisson, RNR) and also gunnery exercises. (10)
19 Oct 1941 HMS Thrasher (Lt. H.S. Mackenzie, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. These included A/S exercises with and practice attacks on HMS Beryl. (11)

29 Dec 1941 HMS Upholder, with Lt. C.P. Norman, RN temporary in command (to give Lt.Cdr. Wanklyn a rest during her next patrol) and HMS Urge (with Lt. J.D. Martin, RN temporary in command (to give Lt.Cdr. Tomkinson a rest during her next patrol) conducted exercises off Malta with HMS Beryl.

When returning from the exercises around 1630 B/29, they were attacked by German Me 109 fighter aircraft of 6 JG/53. HMS Urge dived fairly quickly but some shells hit the casing causing some minor damage, but on HMS Upholder the crew on the bridge were reading a signal from a shore signal station and did not notice the approaching aircraft in time (the message was to warn Upholder about the approaching aircraft). Lt. Norman was hit by gunfire from one of the German fighters and was wounded. Despite his wounds he managed to shut the top hatch as Upholder crash dived. When Upholder arrived back at Malta he was taken to hospital and Lt.Cdr. Wanklyn assumed command again, his rest during Upholders next patrol being cancelled.
7 Oct 1942 At 1335 A/7, HMS P 35 (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, RN) departed Malta for her 12th war patrol (11th in the Mediterranean). She was ordered to patrol in the southern Adriatic.
P 35 first patrolled in the Cape Dukato - Corfu area and only went north through the Otranto Straits on the 12th after HMS P 211 (Cdr. B. Bryant, DSC, RN) left the Adriatic.
She was escorted out until 1705 A/7 by HMS Speedy (Lt. J.G. Brookes, DSC, RN). Before they parted company HMS P 35 made a practice attack on HMS Beryl (?) which was also in the area. (13)
14 Jan 1943 At 1015 A/14, HMS P 45 (Lt. H.B. Turner, RN) departed Malta for her 6th war patrol (4th in the Mediterranean).
P 45 was to proceed through position 36°03'N, 14°00'E thence direct to the patrol area west of line 340° for Kerkennah No.4 Buoy and north of latitude 35°50'N.

Between 1255 A/14 and 1520 A/14, A/S exercises were carried out with HMS Hebe (Lt.Cdr. G. Mowatt, RD, RNR), HMS Rye (Lt. J.A. Pearson, DSC and Bar, RNR) and HMS Beryl (T/S.Lt. H. Olsberg, RNR) on which also practice attacks were made.
[No log is available for this period so no map can be displayed.] (14)
24 Jun 1943 Around 1140 B/24, HMS Unison (Lt. A.R. Daniell, DSC, RN) ended her 16th war patrol (14th in the Mediterranean) at Malta.
She was escorted in by HMS Beryl (T/Lt. H. Olsberg, RNR) which had joined around 0745 B/24. (15)
18 Jul 1943 Around 1400 B/18, HMS Unbroken (Lt. B.J.B. Andrew, DSC, RN) ended her 19th war patrol (also 19th in the Mediterranean) at Malta.
She, and HMS Uproar (Lt. L.E. Herrick, DSC, RN), were escorted in by HMS Beryl (T/Lt. H. Olsberg, RNR), which had joined around 1005 B/18. (16)
24 Jul 1943 At 1145 B/24, HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN) ended her 19th war patrol (also 19th in the Mediterranean) at Malta.
She was escorted in by HMS Beryl (T/Lt. H. Olsberg, RNR), which had joined around 0900 B/24. (17)
29 Aug 1943 At 1200 B/29, HMS Unrivalled (Lt. H.B. Turner, DSC, RN) ended her 15th war patrol (13th in the Mediterranean) at Malta.
She was escorted in by HMS Beryl (T/Lt. H. Olsberg, RNR) which had joined at 0830 B/29. (18)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
